prompt:
The label-print relay wedges in production whenever a single print job throws. Symptom: run() never returns — the coordinator sits there forever waiting on its result queue, we see N-1 'done' reports arrive and then nothing, and the whole service has to be killed. On top of the hang, the job that blew up just vanishes: no error outcome, nothing in the results, we only know it existed from the printer-side logs. With one worker it's even worse — the job queued *behind* the bad one never runs at all.

Expected contract for relaypool.py: every submitted job produces exactly one outcome — ("ok", id, result) or ("error", id, message) — a handler exception is an outcome, not a pool-killer; each worker reports done exactly once so run() always returns; workers keep draining the queue after a bad job; and when run() returns there are no worker threads still alive and nothing left sitting in the queues.

`python3 test_relaypool.py` drives scripted scenarios in separate processes with a 5s hard kill, so on current code you'll see the failure scenarios die on the timeout. Fix relaypool.py. scenarios.py and the tests are the harness — hands off both.
